text: These words are not in the original; they have been inserted to elucidate the train of thought, which is here much confused. It seems probable that Anna meant, ‘Why should you make such a point of being honest, when Providence has given you such a poor reward for being charitable?’

In this chapter the Hebrew and Greek versions still give the story in the first person. But here they correspond more nearly with the Vulgate Latin (and with the Aramaic text from which it was translated); the chief difference is the omission of verses 12-18.
